title: Processing Frozen Human Blood Samples for SQK-LSK114 Oxford Nanopore DNA Sequencing
author: Abigail Miano-Burkhardt

steps.title: Part 1
steps.instruction: Part 1: Preparing Blood Samples (~30 min for 24 samples)

steps.title: Obtain blood samples from -80C freezer...
steps.instruction: Obtain blood samples from -80C freezer and thaw in water bath at 37 °C for 00:15:00 . Note: This is specifically for 6mL tubes, if starting with only 1mL thaw until warm (~ 00:05:00 )

steps.title: Inversion mix blood 10x immediately before use. Note
steps.instruction: Inversion mix blood 10x immediately before use. Note: The blood sample needs to be very thoroughly mixed in order to ensure better DNA isolation. Note: We will be taking 1 mL forward. If your samples have a volume larger than 1 mL , aliquot 1 mL out into 1.5mL Eppendorf DNA LoBind tubes and keep frozen at -80 °C until ready to use. It is important to limit the amount of freeze/thaw cycles as much as possible, the protocol will work best at the first freeze/thaw cycle.

steps.title: Part 2
steps.instruction: Part 2: KingFisher Apex Nanobind HMW DNA Extraction protocol (~3 hours for 24 samples) Note: Protocol is adapted from PacBio Nanobind HT HMW DNA Extraction 1mL Whole Blood KingFisher Apex protocol: https://www.pacb.com/wp-content/uploads/Procedure-checklist-Extracting-HMW-DNA-using-Nanobind-HT-1-mL-blood-kit-for-mammalian-whole-blood-on-KingFisher-Apex-system.pdf Note: The KingFisher Apex protocol can be run with up to 24 samples at once.

steps.title: from PacBio
steps.instruction: from PacBio. Prepare the KingFisher 24 deep well plates as follows: Plate

steps.title: Elution
steps.instruction: Elution: 200 µL Buffer EB Plate

steps.title: 2000 µL Buffer CW2 Plate
steps.instruction: 2000 µL Buffer CW2 Plate

steps.title: 2000 µL Buffer CW2 Plate
steps.instruction: 2000 µL Buffer CW2 Plate

steps.title: CW1 Wash
steps.instruction: CW1 Wash: 2000 µL Buffer CW1 Plate

steps.title: Nanobind storage
steps.instruction: Nanobind storage: 5mm Nanobind Disks Plate

steps.title: Lysis Binding
steps.instruction: Lysis Binding: 100 µL Proteinase K + 1000 µL Whole Blood + 100 µL RNase A + 750 µL Buffer BL3 Note: Add BL3 gently against side of well. Adding BL3 directly to the solution may affect extraction performance. Plate

steps.title: Comb tip
steps.instruction: Comb tip: 24 Flex tip comb Note: Sample and reagents in lysis binding plate must be added to the plate in the order listed above.

steps.title: Run Kingfisher program
steps.instruction: Run Kingfisher program: 1mL_Blood_Nanobind_HT_APEX script (102-998-100).

steps.title: After ~ 00:50:00 when the program...
steps.instruction: After ~ 00:50:00 when the program pauses, add 1500 µL isopropyl alcohol (IPA) to lysis binding plate. Note: Add IPA gently against side of well. Adding IPA directly to solution may affect extraction purity.

steps.title: When the program ends after ~...
steps.instruction: When the program ends after ~ 01:45:00 (from the start), transfer eluate to a 1.5mL Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube. Note: The program is designed to leave the nanobind disk in the elution plate. If the nanobind disk ends up in the tip comb plate, this does not affect extraction performance. Use a P200 to remove any elution buffer remaining on the nanobind disk.

steps.title: Let samples rest overnight at room temperature (RT)
steps.instruction: Let samples rest overnight at room temperature (RT).

steps.title: After overnight rest at RT, DNA...
steps.instruction: After overnight rest at RT, DNA can be stored at 4 °C for up to four weeks, or -80 °C indefinitely.

steps.title: Part 3
steps.instruction: Part 3: Pre-Size Selection DNA Quantification (~30 min for 16 samples) Note: This duration does not include the time it takes to size the samples. The total time it takes to size 16 samples on the TapeStation or the Femto Pulse is ~45 minutes or ~3 hours and 30 minutes, respectively.

steps.title: Following overnight rest, pipette mix samples 10x
steps.instruction: Following overnight rest, pipette mix samples 10x.

steps.title: Quantify by taking a single measurement...
steps.instruction: Quantify by taking a single measurement on the Qubit Flex Fluorometer.

steps.title: Size on the Agilent Tapestation 4200...
steps.instruction: Size on the Agilent Tapestation 4200 or Agilent Femto Pulse System. The expected size range for samples post-extraction is >80kb. Figure 2. Example TapeStation trace pre-size selection Figure 3. Example Femto Pulse trace pre-size selection Note: I t is recommended to use the Femto Pulse System to get a better representation of the size distribution of the DNA samples.

steps.title: Once all the samples are properly...
steps.instruction: Once all the samples are properly quantified, move on to size selection. Note: Blood DNA recoveries after KingFisher are very variable per sample and per run. If recovery is very low (<5ug) repeat Parts 1-3 with 2 x 1 mL of sample in question. If either of the extra runs has a good recovery, move on with that DNA. If all three are bad, you can pool them together to reach DNA target amount.

steps.title: Part 4
steps.instruction: Part 4: Size Selection (~2 hours for 16 samples) Note: If you have to pool multiple 1 mL runs for a sample, it is possible that the concentration will be <40ng/uL. If it is lower, a clean up step here may be used to re-elute the pool into a smaller volume to increase the concentration.

steps.title: Ensure your samples are between 40-150ng/uL
steps.instruction: Ensure your samples are between 40-150ng/uL. More concentrated samples will need dilution with TE buffer or water. Size selection is not recommended for samples below 40ng/uL. Note: TE buffer is preferred for long-term storage since it reduces DNA degradation over time.

steps.title: Start a microcentrifuge at 10,000 x...
steps.instruction: Start a microcentrifuge at 10,000 x g and 25 °C for 00:05:00 while preparing the samples.

steps.title: In the meantime, place the necessary...
steps.instruction: In the meantime, place the necessary sample volume for at least 6ug into a 1.5mL Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube. Note: Expect to lose 40-60% of material during size-selection. Do not take anymore than 20ug into size selection.

steps.title: We will be using the PacBio...
steps.instruction: We will be using the PacBio Short Read Eliminator Kit (102-208-300). To each sample, add equal volume of Buffer SRE.

steps.title: Mix thoroughly by flicking the tube
steps.instruction: Mix thoroughly by flicking the tube.

steps.title: Load tube into the microcentrifuge with...
steps.instruction: Load tube into the microcentrifuge with hinge facing out and make sure samples are balanced. Note: Inserting tube with the hinge out is crucial to avoid aspirating the pellet if not visible in later steps. Figure 4. Taken from Circulomics Short Read Eliminator Kit Family Handbook v2.0 (07/2019) a) note orientation of the tube in centrifuge. Pellet will form on the side of the tube facing outwards at the bottom of the tube. b) Pipette from opposite side of tube on the thumb lip side to avoid disturbing pellet (pellet may not be visible).

steps.title: Centrifuge at 10,000 x g for 00:30:00 at 25 °C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 10,000 x g for 00:30:00 at 25 °C .

steps.title: Remove the first set of eight...
steps.instruction: Remove the first set of eight tubes (Set 1) from the centrifuge, repositioning the second set of eight tubes (Set 2) in the centrifuge so they are balanced. Spin Set 2 at 10,000 x g for another ~ 00:05:00 at 25 °C .

steps.title: For very viscous DNA, the pellet...
steps.instruction: For very viscous DNA, the pellet may not initially form tightly and can lead to easy aspiration of DNA. If this is the case, remove as much supernatant as possible but leave behind what you cannot pipette without aspirating DNA. Note: Pellet will be tighter after the ethanol (EtOH) wash.

steps.title: Carefully add 200 µL of fresh...
steps.instruction: Carefully add 200 µL of fresh 70% EtOH on the opposite side of the hinge wall. Remove Set 2 from the centrifuge and place Set 1 in the centrifuge. Centrifuge at 10,000 x g for ~ 00:05:00 at 25 °C . Note: Do not mix after adding EtOH.

steps.title: Carefully remove EtOH wash from Set...
steps.instruction: Carefully remove EtOH wash from Set 1 without disturbing the pellet.

steps.title: Add 50-100uL of Buffer EB and...
steps.instruction: Add 50-100uL of Buffer EB and let incubate at RT for 00:20:00 . Note: Letting the sample incubate for longer may better homogenize the sample and may give better quantification readings.

steps.title: After incubation, flick the tube to ensure proper mixing
steps.instruction: After incubation, flick the tube to ensure proper mixing.

steps.title: Part 5
steps.instruction: Part 5: Post-Size Selection DNA quantification (~10 min for 8 samples)

steps.title: Analyze size selection recoveries via Qubit. Note
steps.instruction: Analyze size selection recoveries via Qubit. Note: Quantification post-size selection can be difficult. If necessary, gently pipette mix the sample 10x before quantification to homogenize. The most accurate quantification will come after Megaruptor shearing in Part 6, but an estimate of DNA concentration is important in this step to standardize the samples going into shearing.

steps.title: Part 6
steps.instruction: Part 6: Shearing (~3 hours for 8 samples)

steps.title: We will be using the Diagenode...
steps.instruction: We will be using the Diagenode Megaruptor 3 Shearing Kit (E07010003). In a Megaruptor 3 shearing tube, make up the size selected sample to 150 µL and 40-70ng/uL with TE Buffer or water. Note: It is ok if target concentration cannot be reached for all samples. If concentration differs from this range, make sure the Megaruptor 3 shearing settings are updated to reflect your sample concentration. If shearing more than one sample try to get all the concentrations to be as close as possible to this target range and/or to each other. Note: We recommend taking in a larger concentration into shearing since you will lose some sample when removing the syringe post-shear. If the sample concentration is higher than 70ng/uL post-SRE but diluting it with TE buffer or water to 150uL would make it less than 70ng/uL, it is suggested to dilute to 100uL rather than 150uL. If the sample concentration does not reach 70ng/uL post-SRE, it is suggested to take all sample into shearing and do not dilute with TE buffer or water. Make sure Megaruptor 3 shearing settings reflect your exact sample volume.

steps.title: Attach the Megaruptor 3 shearing syringe...
steps.instruction: Attach the Megaruptor 3 shearing syringe onto the tube and push the entire item into the MR3 slots until it fits snugly. If running fewer than 8 samples, put the tubes in the 1st and/or 8th slots, working your way in. Note: Samples should always be balanced. If running an odd number of samples, samples can be balanced with an empty corresponding tube.

steps.title: Shear twice at speed 20 (takes...
steps.instruction: Shear twice at speed 20 (takes ~ 03:00:00 if the MR3 is set to 150uL volume).

steps.title: Once finished, remove the sample from...
steps.instruction: Once finished, remove the sample from the Megaruptor 3 and carefully remove the syringe from the tube. Make sure the plunger is fully depressed in order to avoid losing sample. Use a P200 pipette to aspirate any leftover sample on the syringe.

steps.title: Avoid any vortexing of the DNA...
steps.instruction: Avoid any vortexing of the DNA from this point on to avoid any unnecessary further shearing. Instead mix by gently flicking the tube and spin down.

steps.title: Part 7
steps.instruction: Part 7: Post-Shear DNA Quantification (~10 min for 8 samples)

steps.title: Quantify the samples on the Qubit...
steps.instruction: Quantify the samples on the Qubit and size using the Agilent TapeStation 4200 or the Agilent Femto Pulse System. Although the targeted size is 30kb, the observed size range for samples post-shear is between 30-70kb. These usually still sequence around 30kb. Figure 5. Example TapeStation trace post-size selection and shearing Figure 6. Example Femto Pulse trace post-size selection and shearing

steps.title: Upload the TapeStation/Femto reports and quantifications to tracking google sheet
steps.instruction: Upload the TapeStation/Femto reports and quantifications to tracking google sheet.

steps.title: At this point, at least 2
steps.instruction: At this point, at least 2.5 µg of DNA is necessary to move on to library prep.

steps.title: The DNA can be stored at...
steps.instruction: The DNA can be stored at 4 °C for up to four weeks, or -80 °C indefinitely.

steps.title: Manual SQK-LSK114 Library Prep and Sequencing...
steps.instruction: Manual SQK-LSK114 Library Prep and Sequencing (~6 hours, including reloads) Note: Library prep can also be done on the Hamilton NGS Star and can process 48 samples in ~4 hours using the HAMILTON NGST STAR Oxford Nanopore SQK-LSK114 Library preparation SOP: https://dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.n2bvj36mnlk5/v1 Note: Reagents are from Oxford Nanopore Ligation Sequencing Kit V14 (SQK-LSK114). Library prep is following the standard Oxford Nanopore Ligation Sequencing DNA V14 (SQK-LSK

steps.title: protocol with minor tweaks
steps.instruction: protocol with minor tweaks: https://community.nanoporetech.com/docs/prepare/library_prep_protocols/genomic-dna-by-ligation-sqk-lsk114/v/gde_9161_v114_revo_29jun2022?devices=promethion Minor tweaks: 2.5ug DNA library in 48 µL (Part A

steps.title: Add 45 µL of AMPure XP beads (Part B
steps.instruction: Add 45 µL of AMPure XP beads (Part B

steps.title: Use Short Fragment Buffer (Part B
steps.instruction: Use Short Fragment Buffer (Part B

steps.title: Incubate for 00:20:00 at 37 °C...
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:20:00 at 37 °C and 300-450 x g (Part B Step 18)

steps.title: Mix thoroughly by gently flicking the...
steps.instruction: Mix thoroughly by gently flicking the tube or very gently pipetting up and down 10x, and then spin down.

steps.title: Using a Thermocycler, incubate samples at...
steps.instruction: Using a Thermocycler, incubate samples at 20 °C for 00:05:00 and then 65 °C for 00:05:00 . Note: Start and pause Thermocycler to allow lid to come to 85 °C before putting samples in.

steps.title: Resuspend the AMPure XP beads by vortexing
steps.instruction: Resuspend the AMPure XP beads by vortexing.

steps.title: Transfer DNA samples to clean 1
steps.instruction: Transfer DNA samples to clean 1.5mL Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube.

steps.title: Add 60 µL (or equivalent volume, see
steps.instruction: Add 60 µL (or equivalent volume, see

steps.title: of resuspended beads to the reaction...
steps.instruction: of resuspended beads to the reaction and mix by flicking the tube 10x. Do not pipette mix here as beads may clump around the pipette tip.

steps.title: Incubate on a rocking laboratory shaker...
steps.instruction: Incubate on a rocking laboratory shaker or hula mixer for 00:05:00 at RT.

steps.title: Prepare 500 µL per sample of...
steps.instruction: Prepare 500 µL per sample of fresh 80% ethanol in nuclease-free water.

steps.title: Spin down and pellet sample on...
steps.instruction: Spin down and pellet sample on magnet until eluate is clear and colorless, about 00:02:00

steps.title: Keep the tube on the magnet...
steps.instruction: Keep the tube on the magnet and pipette off the supernatant. Note: Can retain if needed just in case the final elution quantification is uncharacteristically low.

steps.title: With the samples remaining on the...
steps.instruction: With the samples remaining on the magnet, wash the beads with 200 µL of 80% EtOH, pipetting on the opposite wall making sure not to disturb the pellet. Once EtOH has been added to all samples, immediately remove and discard the EtOH. Note: The goal here is to make sure the beads are fully covered. If initial volume of beads was significantly higher than 60uL, more EtOH may be required.

steps.title: Spin down and place the tube...
steps.instruction: Spin down and place the tube back on the magnet, pipetting off any residual EtOH.

steps.title: Allow to dry for ~ 00:00:30...
steps.instruction: Allow to dry for ~ 00:00:30 but do not overdry to the point of cracking.

steps.title: Remove the tube from the magnetic...
steps.instruction: Remove the tube from the magnetic rack and resuspend the pellet in 62 µL nuclease-free water. Incubate for 00:03:00 at RT, gently flicking every so often. If not quantifying the sample post-DNA repair and end-prep, user can resuspend the pellet in 60uL water.

steps.title: Spin down and pellet the samples...
steps.instruction: Spin down and pellet the samples on a magnet until eluate is clear and colorless.

steps.title: Remove and retain 62 µL of eluate into a 1
steps.instruction: Remove and retain 62 µL of eluate into a 1.5mL Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube.

steps.title: Optional
steps.instruction: Optional: Quantify 2uL of sample on the Qubit. Note: It is possible to store samples at 4 °C overnight at this step if necessary.

steps.title: Spin down the Ligation Adapter (LA)...
steps.instruction: Spin down the Ligation Adapter (LA) and NEBNext Quick T4 DNA Ligase, then return to ice. Note: Do not allow LA or Quick T4 to remain at room temperature for too long. Since LA and Quick T4 do not freeze at -20 °C , it is possible to leave them in the freezer until needed.

steps.title: Thaw Ligation Buffer (LNB) at RT,...
steps.instruction: Thaw Ligation Buffer (LNB) at RT, mix by pipetting up and down (vortexing is ineffective due to viscosity), and place on ice.

steps.title: Thaw Elution Buffer (EB) and Short...
steps.instruction: Thaw Elution Buffer (EB) and Short Fragment Buffer (SFB) at RT, mix by vortexing, spin down, and place on ice.

steps.title: In a 1
steps.instruction: In a 1.5mL Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube, mix the following in order: 60 µL DNA sample 25 µL LNB 10 µL Quick T4 5 µL LA

steps.title: Mix by gently pipetting and spin down
steps.instruction: Mix by gently pipetting and spin down.

steps.title: Incubate the reaction for 00:10:00 at RT
steps.instruction: Incubate the reaction for 00:10:00 at RT.

steps.title: During this time, put flow cells out at RT
steps.instruction: During this time, put flow cells out at RT.

steps.title: Resuspend AMPure beads by vortexing
steps.instruction: Resuspend AMPure beads by vortexing.

steps.title: Add 45 µL of resuspended beads...
steps.instruction: Add 45 µL of resuspended beads to the reaction and mix by flicking.

steps.title: Incubate on a rocking laboratory shaker...
steps.instruction: Incubate on a rocking laboratory shaker or hula mixer for 00:05:00 at RT.

steps.title: Spin down sample and pellet on magnet
steps.instruction: Spin down sample and pellet on magnet.

steps.title: Keeping tube on magnet, pipette off the supernatant. Note
steps.instruction: Keeping tube on magnet, pipette off the supernatant. Note: Can retain supernatant if needed just in case the final elution quantification is uncharacteristically low.

steps.title: Wash the beads with 250 µL...
steps.instruction: Wash the beads with 250 µL SFB, remove from magnet and flick to resuspend, spin down and re-pellet on magnet, and then remove and discard supernatant.

steps.title: Spin down and place the tube...
steps.instruction: Spin down and place the tube back on magnet, pipetting off any residual supernatant.

steps.title: Allow to dry for ~ 00:00:30...
steps.instruction: Allow to dry for ~ 00:00:30 , but do not overdry to the point of cracking.

steps.title: Remove the tube from magnet and...
steps.instruction: Remove the tube from magnet and resuspend pellet in 26 µL EB, spin down, and incubate for 00:20:00 at 37 °C and 300-450 x g.

steps.title: During this time, QC the flow cells. Note
steps.instruction: During this time, QC the flow cells. Note: Wait at least 00:20:00 after taking out the flow cells to allow them to reach RT before loading onto the PromethION to avoid condensation formation. Note: If trying to reach 30x coverage, it is recommended to only use flow cells with >7000 pores.

steps.title: Pellet the beads on a magnet...
steps.instruction: Pellet the beads on a magnet until eluate is clear and colorless.

steps.title: Remove and retain 26 µL of...
steps.instruction: Remove and retain 26 µL of eluate into a clean 1.5mL Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube, being careful not to aspirate any beads (this is the DNA library).

steps.title: Quantify 2uL of sample on the Qubit
steps.instruction: Quantify 2uL of sample on the Qubit.

steps.title: Re-prep library from Part 6 if <540ng
steps.instruction: Re-prep library from Part 6 if <540ng. We have calculated this based on a DNA size of 30kb and at a coverage of 30x over three loads. If your sample differs from this size, recalculate based on 10-20 fmol per load. Note: 540ng will be enough for three loads of 180ng each, which is the amount that our testing has indicated is necessary to hit 30x coverage. If necessary, user can load as low as 100ng with minimal affect on pore occupancy.

steps.title: Keep libraries on ice until ready...
steps.instruction: Keep libraries on ice until ready to load on flow cell.

steps.title: Repeat steps 4 and
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 4 and

steps.title: Gently pipette mix the prepared library mix right before loading
steps.instruction: Gently pipette mix the prepared library mix right before loading.

steps.title: Load 200 µL of the library...
steps.instruction: Load 200 µL of the library mix into inlet port 1 on the flow cell.

steps.title: Close valve to seal inlet port and close PromethION door
steps.instruction: Close valve to seal inlet port and close PromethION door.

steps.title: Wait 00:10:00 and then initiate sequencing
steps.instruction: Wait 00:10:00 and then initiate sequencing.

steps.title: Ideally, the library quants yielded at...
steps.instruction: Ideally, the library quants yielded at least 540ng to allow for 3 x 180ng loads, the latter 2 loaded approximately after 24 and 48 hours. However this will vary slightly depending on pore usage, data generated, as well as other factors (i.e., if after 24 hours there are still 3000+ pores then the sample does not need to be reloaded until 48 hours). To wash and reload a flow cell, begin by thawing Wash Mix (WMX) on ice and Wash Diluent (DIL) at RT Note: DIL should be vortexed. WMX should NOT be vortexed, only spun. In a new tube, add 2 µL WMX to 398 µL DIL and pipette mix. This is your flow cell wash mix. Pause the PromethION runs and export .pdf run reports. With inlet port 1 closed, remove waste from port 2 or

steps.title: Rotate the inlet port 1 cover to reveal inlet port
steps.instruction: Rotate the inlet port 1 cover to reveal inlet port

steps.title: Using a P1000, insert tip into...
steps.instruction: Using a P1000, insert tip into inlet port 1 and draw back a small volume using the wheel to remove any air bubbles (usually around 20-30 μL, just until a small volume enters the pipette tip). Load 400 µL flow cell wash mix into inlet port 1, avoiding any introduction of air. Wait 01:00:00 Repeat priming steps and reload samples (steps 1 - 13).

steps.title: D
steps.instruction: D. Flushing and Recycling Flow Cells (~15 min per set of 4 flow cells) Following the completion of the sequencing, flow cells may be removed from the sequencer. Place enough absorbent material to take up approximately 4 mL of flush waste. Rotate valve to reveal inlet port 1. Place flow cell at a 45° angle on the absorbent material and, using a P1000, flush 1 mL of DI water into the inlet port. Repeat 3 more times for a total of 4 mL. Once complete, close the inlet port cover and remove all liquid from the waste port. Dispose of absorbent material as local biological waste guidelines dictate. Return flow cells to clear plastic tray in which it was shipped. Put the clear plastic lid back on the tray. Place the tray back in the packaging. Place packaged cells in the returns box (large box can hold up to 80 flow cells or 20 packs). Once returns box is filled, follow the instructions here and follow the prompts to request the box to be sent back to Nanopore.

steps.title: Following 72 hours of sequencing the...
steps.instruction: Following 72 hours of sequencing the sample should yield an N50 ~30kb with a data output ~100Gb. Figure 6. Expected Read Length Histogram Figure 7. Expected Muxscan (three loads)

steps.title: Pipette mix samples 10x to homogenize
steps.instruction: Pipette mix samples 10x to homogenize.

steps.title: While Set 2 is spinning, carefully...
steps.instruction: While Set 2 is spinning, carefully remove the supernatant without disturbing the DNA pellet from Set 1. Pellet may not always be visible but will have formed on the bottom of the tube under the hinge region. Pipette from the opposite wall (thumb lip side of tube).

steps.title: Repeat steps 4
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 4.15 and 4.16 for Set 2 once it is done spinning.

steps.title: While Set 1 is spinning, carefully...
steps.instruction: While Set 1 is spinning, carefully remove the supernatant without disturbing the DNA pellet from Set 2.

steps.title: Carefully add 200 µL of fresh...
steps.instruction: Carefully add 200 µL of fresh 70% EtOH on the opposite side of the hinge wall. Remove Set 1 from the centrifuge and place Set 2 in the centrifuge. Centrifuge at 10,000 x g for ~ 00:05:00 at 25 °C .

steps.title: During this time, prepare 250 µL...
steps.instruction: During this time, prepare 250 µL per sample of fresh 70% EtOH in nuclease-free water.
